rawText:
The race to dominate artificial intelligence has devolved into a bruising three-way contest among OpenAI, Anthropic, and the SpaceX-xAI merger -- each led by a figure whose personality shapes strategy as much as any balance sheet -- with combined IPO ambitions approaching $3 trillion that would, if realised, dwarf the total sum raised by all American public offerings over the past decade.

Sam Altman, characterised by rivals as a mercenary who exploited Anthropic's public clash with the Pentagon to seize classified defence contracts, has built OpenAI into the largest of the three labs, drawing $13 billion in revenue last year from a sprawling business model that spans 900 million weekly ChatGPT users, tiered consumer subscriptions, and a rapidly expanding enterprise division that now forecasts $30 billion in sales for this year alone.

Dario Amodei, the missionary who left OpenAI to found a safety-first competitor, finds himself in the paradoxical position of running a principled lab that must nonetheless raise enormous sums to survive -- a tension laid bare when his leaked internal memo, written in the heat of Anthropic's standoff with the Trump administration over autonomous-weapons guardrails, called Altman "mendacious" and inadvertently turned the controversy into a marketing triumph that boosted Claude's popularity.

Elon Musk, the messianic figure who helped create OpenAI only to sue it for $134 billion, has merged xAI with SpaceX in a gambit that compensates for his AI lab's comparatively modest $500 million in annual revenue by bolting it onto a rocket company generating $8 billion in operating profit, thereby creating a combined entity that could list at $1.5 trillion and make him history's first trillionaire.

Nvidia, the indispensable arms dealer in this conflict, is meanwhile transforming itself from a chipmaker into what its chief executive Jensen Huang calls a "foundational company" for the AI economy, one that generated $216 billion in revenue last year by controlling more than two-thirds of the world's AI processing power and now plans to sell bundled AI factories, open-source models, and networking equipment across what it describes as a "five-layer cake" of industry infrastructure.

Yet even as American labs wage their war for supremacy, China's internet giants -- Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent -- have opened a second front by handing out $1.2 billion in subsidies during Lunar New Year to lure consumers toward agentic chatbots capable of ordering meals and facilitating transactions, each company dreaming of an AI-powered super app that channels users toward its existing e-commerce and payment services.

The ferocity of this global competition has left investors bewildered, unable to decide whether individual companies are AI winners or losers -- a confusion illustrated by Duolingo's share price doubling and then crashing by 80 percent within eighteen months, by Alphabet swinging from supposed obsolescence to an 85 percent stock surge, and by Goldman Sachs's index of firms most at risk from AI disruption falling more than 20 percent even as broader markets hovered near record highs.

History suggests that such confusion is entirely normal during technological revolutions, since stockmarkets correctly identify structural disruption only about half the time -- failing to foresee Kodak's demise while wrongly predicting the death of tobacco firms on multiple occasions -- and academic research confirms that analysts systematically overestimate the future profitability of companies whose technological base is becoming obsolete.

India's $315-billion IT outsourcing industry, long pegged as the sector most vulnerable to AI-driven displacement, encapsulates this uncertainty perfectly: although tools like Claude Code can now prototype a software application in minutes, the messy reality of legacy systems, undocumented code, and brownfield environments means that clients who expected radical automation often end up hiring as many outsourced engineers as before, while the consultancy arms of firms like Infosys and TCS pivot toward higher-value strategic roles that could be worth $300-400 billion by decade's end.

Whether the ultimate victors of this arms race will be the three lab founders preparing their colossal IPOs, the Chinese giants subsidising their way to dominance, or the already-profitable Alphabet -- which generated $132 billion in net income last year and can fund its AI investments from its own pocket at a comparatively modest valuation of nine times revenue -- remains a question that neither markets nor the technologists building the models can yet answer with any confidence.

overview.title: The AI Arms Race: Who Will Win the Biggest Prize in Business?

sentences.original: The race to dominate artificial intelligence has devolved into a bruising three-way contest among OpenAI, Anthropic, and the SpaceX-xAI merger -- each led by a figure whose personality shapes strategy as much as any balance sheet -- with combined IPO ambitions approaching $3 trillion that would, if realised, dwarf the total sum raised by all American public offerings over the past decade.
sentences.translation: 인공지능 패권을 차지하려는 경쟁은 OpenAI, Anthropic, 그리고 SpaceX-xAI 합병체 사이의 치열한 3자 대결로 변질되었다 -- 각각 재무제표 못지않게 전략을 좌우하는 인물이 이끌고 있으며 -- 합산 IPO 목표 가치는 3조 달러에 육박해, 실현될 경우 지난 10년간 미국 전체 상장으로 조달된 총액을 왜소하게 만들 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The race to dominate artificial intelligence
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has devolved into
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사구

grammarElements.text: a bruising three-way contest among OpenAI, Anthropic, and the SpaceX-xAI merger
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: with combined IPO ambitions approaching $3 trillion that would ... dwarf the total sum
grammarElements.label: 부대상황 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The negotiation devolved into a shouting match within minutes.
The company emerged from a bruising legal battle with its competitors.

expressions.usage: 규모의 압도적 차이를 강조할 때 동사 dwarf를 사용

patterns.explanation: 경쟁이나 상황이 점점 나쁜 방향으로 변질되었음을 표현하는 패턴
examples: The policy debate has devolved into personal attacks.
The partnership has devolved into an acrimonious dispute.

drillQuestions.question: The race to dominate artificial intelligence has ______ into a bruising three-way contest.

sentences.notes: devolved into라는 부정적 뉘앙스의 동사로 시작해 AI 경쟁의 격화를 프레이밍한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The race to dominate artificial intelligence
a bruising three-way contest
combined IPO ambitions approaching $3 trillion
dwarf the total sum raised by all American public offerings
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 전체 상장 조달 총액을 왜소하게 만들다

sentences.original: Sam Altman, characterised by rivals as a mercenary who exploited Anthropic's public clash with the Pentagon to seize classified defence contracts, has built OpenAI into the largest of the three labs, drawing $13 billion in revenue last year from a sprawling business model that spans 900 million weekly ChatGPT users, tiered consumer subscriptions, and a rapidly expanding enterprise division that now forecasts $30 billion in sales for this year alone.
sentences.translation: 라이벌들에 의해 Anthropic과 국방부의 공개적 충돌을 이용해 기밀 방위 계약을 차지한 용병으로 묘사되는 샘 올트먼은 OpenAI를 세 연구소 중 가장 큰 곳으로 키웠으며, 주간 9억 명의 ChatGPT 사용자와 단계별 소비자 구독, 그리고 올해만 300억 달러 매출을 예측하는 급속히 확장 중인 기업용 사업부를 아우르는 방대한 사업 모델에서 지난해 130억 달러의 매출을 올렸다.

grammarElements.text: Sam Altman
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: characterised by rivals as a mercenary who exploited ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: has built
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: drawing $13 billion in revenue ... from a sprawling business model that spans ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Critics dismissed him as a mercenary with no genuine commitment to safety.

expressions.usage: 사업 범위가 여러 영역에 걸쳐 확장되었음을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Person], characterised by [group] as [label], has built [entity] into [status]
patterns.explanation: 인물 소개와 동시에 외부 평가를 삽입하는 인물 프로필 패턴
examples: The CEO, characterised by regulators as reckless, has built the startup into a market leader.
The minister, characterised by critics as an ideologue, has built the programme into the government's flagship policy.

drillQuestions.question: Sam Altman has built OpenAI into the largest of the three labs, drawing $13 billion in revenue from a ______ business model.

sentences.notes: 과거분사 삽입구로 외부 평가를 끼워넣고, 분사구문으로 매출 구조를 한 문장에 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: characterised by rivals as a mercenary
has built OpenAI into the largest of the three labs
koreanAlignment.ko: OpenAI를 세 연구소 중 가장 큰 곳으로 키웠다

koreanAlignment.en: a sprawling business model that spans ...
a rapidly expanding enterprise division

sentences.original: Dario Amodei, the missionary who left OpenAI to found a safety-first competitor, finds himself in the paradoxical position of running a principled lab that must nonetheless raise enormous sums to survive -- a tension laid bare when his leaked internal memo, written in the heat of Anthropic's standoff with the Trump administration over autonomous-weapons guardrails, called Altman "mendacious" and inadvertently turned the controversy into a marketing triumph that boosted Claude's popularity.
sentences.translation: 안전 우선 경쟁사를 세우기 위해 OpenAI를 떠난 선교사적 인물 다리오 아모데이는, 원칙적 연구소를 운영하면서도 생존을 위해 막대한 자금을 모아야 하는 역설적 위치에 처해 있다 -- 자율무기 안전장치를 둘러싼 Anthropic과 트럼프 행정부의 대치 와중에 작성된 그의 유출된 내부 메모가 올트먼을 '거짓말쟁이'라고 부르며 의도치 않게 논란을 Claude의 인기를 끌어올린 마케팅 승리로 바꿔버렸을 때 그 긴장이 적나라하게 드러났다.

grammarElements.text: Dario Amodei, the missionary who left OpenAI to found a safety-first competitor
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 관계절

grammarElements.text: finds himself in the paradoxical position of
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: running a principled lab that must nonetheless raise enormous sums
grammarElements.label: 전치사 목적어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: a tension laid bare when his leaked internal memo ... called Altman "mendacious"
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 부연절

vocabulary.example: The report described the executive's testimony as mendacious and self-serving.

expressions.usage: 감춰진 진실이나 긴장이 외부에 노출될 때 사용하는 강한 표현

patterns.pattern: [Person] finds himself/herself in the paradoxical position of [doing A] that must [also do B]
patterns.explanation: 한 인물이 상충하는 두 가지를 동시에 해야 하는 역설적 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The regulator finds herself in the paradoxical position of promoting innovation that must also be constrained.
The charity finds itself in the paradoxical position of spending on fundraising that must also reduce overhead.

drillQuestions.question: Dario Amodei finds himself in the ______ position of running a principled lab that must raise enormous sums.

sentences.notes: paradoxical position이 아모데이의 딜레마를 압축하고, 대시 뒤 일화가 그 역설의 실제 사례를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: the missionary who left OpenAI to found a safety-first competitor
koreanAlignment.ko: 안전 우선 경쟁사를 세우기 위해 OpenAI를 떠난 선교사적 인물

koreanAlignment.en: the paradoxical position of running a principled lab
a tension laid bare
inadvertently turned the controversy into a marketing triumph
koreanAlignment.ko: 의도치 않게 논란을 마케팅 승리로 바꿨다

sentences.original: Elon Musk, the messianic figure who helped create OpenAI only to sue it for $134 billion, has merged xAI with SpaceX in a gambit that compensates for his AI lab's comparatively modest $500 million in annual revenue by bolting it onto a rocket company generating $8 billion in operating profit, thereby creating a combined entity that could list at $1.5 trillion and make him history's first trillionaire.
sentences.translation: OpenAI를 공동 창립한 뒤 1,340억 달러 소송을 건 메시아적 인물 일론 머스크는, 연간 매출 5억 달러에 불과한 자신의 AI 연구소를 영업이익 80억 달러를 내는 로켓 회사에 결합시키는 승부수를 통해 xAI를 SpaceX와 합병했고, 이로써 1.5조 달러에 상장하여 역사상 최초의 조만장자가 될 수 있는 통합 기업체를 만들어냈다.

grammarElements.text: Elon Musk, the messianic figure who helped create OpenAI only to sue it
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 관계절

grammarElements.text: has merged
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: xAI with SpaceX
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: in a gambit that compensates for ... by bolting it onto ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절 (compensate for A by B)

vocabulary.example: The merger was widely seen as a high-risk gambit to leapfrog rivals.

expressions.usage: 기존 구조에 새 요소를 급하게 접합하는 뉘앙스로, 다소 비판적 톤을 내포

patterns.pattern: [Person] has [action] in a gambit that compensates for [weakness] by [strategy]
patterns.explanation: 약점을 보완하기 위한 전략적 행보를 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The CEO has restructured the division in a gambit that compensates for declining sales by cutting fixed costs.
The bank has acquired the fintech in a gambit that compensates for its weak digital platform by buying market share.

drillQuestions.question: Elon Musk has merged xAI with SpaceX in a ______ that compensates for his AI lab's modest revenue.

sentences.notes: only to 구문이 머스크의 아이러니한 궤적을 보여주고, gambit이 합병의 투기적 성격을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: the messianic figure who helped create OpenAI only to sue it
koreanAlignment.ko: OpenAI를 공동 창립한 뒤 소송을 건 메시아적 인물

koreanAlignment.en: in a gambit that compensates for his AI lab's comparatively modest revenue
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 연구소의 상대적으로 미미한 매출을 보완하는 승부수

koreanAlignment.en: bolting it onto a rocket company
history's first trillionaire

sentences.original: Nvidia, the indispensable arms dealer in this conflict, is meanwhile transforming itself from a chipmaker into what its chief executive Jensen Huang calls a "foundational company" for the AI economy, one that generated $216 billion in revenue last year by controlling more than two-thirds of the world's AI processing power and now plans to sell bundled AI factories, open-source models, and networking equipment across what it describes as a "five-layer cake" of industry infrastructure.
sentences.translation: 이 갈등에서 없어서는 안 될 무기상인 Nvidia는 한편으로 칩 제조사에서 CEO 젠슨 황이 AI 경제의 '기초 기업'이라 부르는 존재로 변신 중이다. 지난해 전 세계 AI 처리 능력의 3분의 2 이상을 장악해 2,160억 달러의 매출을 올렸으며, 이제 자사가 산업 인프라의 '5층 케이크'라 묘사하는 영역 전반에 걸쳐 통합 AI 공장과 오픈소스 모델, 네트워킹 장비를 판매할 계획이다.

grammarElements.text: Nvidia, the indispensable arms dealer in this conflict
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격

grammarElements.text: is transforming itself
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사

grammarElements.text: from a chipmaker into what ... calls a "foundational company"
grammarElements.label: from A into B + 명사절

grammarElements.text: one that generated $216 billion ... and now plans to sell ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절

vocabulary.example: GPU chips have become indispensable to every major AI lab.

expressions.usage: 비유적으로 경쟁 당사자들 모두에게 핵심 자원을 공급하는 기업을 가리킴

patterns.pattern: [Company] is transforming itself from [old identity] into [new identity]
patterns.explanation: 기업의 근본적 정체성 전환을 표현하는 패턴
examples: The publisher is transforming itself from a print house into a digital-first media company.
The automaker is transforming itself from an engine manufacturer into a mobility platform.

drillQuestions.question: Nvidia is transforming itself from a chipmaker into what Jensen Huang calls a "______ company" for the AI economy.

sentences.notes: 'arms dealer'라는 비유가 Nvidia의 독특한 위치 -- 모든 진영에 필수 자원을 파는 존재 -- 를 단번에 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the indispensable arms dealer in this conflict
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 갈등에서 없어서는 안 될 무기상

koreanAlignment.en: transforming itself from a chipmaker into a foundational company
controlling more than two-thirds of the world's AI processing power
koreanAlignment.ko: 전 세계 AI 처리 능력의 3분의 2 이상 장악

koreanAlignment.en: a five-layer cake of industry infrastructure

sentences.original: Yet even as American labs wage their war for supremacy, China's internet giants -- Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent -- have opened a second front by handing out $1.2 billion in subsidies during Lunar New Year to lure consumers toward agentic chatbots capable of ordering meals and facilitating transactions, each company dreaming of an AI-powered super app that channels users toward its existing e-commerce and payment services.
sentences.translation: 그러나 미국 연구소들이 패권을 위한 전쟁을 벌이는 바로 그 순간에도, 중국의 인터넷 대기업들 -- 알리바바, 바이트댄스, 텐센트 -- 은 설 연휴 기간 12억 달러의 보조금을 뿌려 식사 주문과 거래 처리가 가능한 에이전트형 챗봇으로 소비자를 끌어들이며 제2전선을 열었고, 각 회사는 사용자를 기존 전자상거래와 결제 서비스로 유도하는 AI 기반 슈퍼앱을 꿈꾸고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Yet even as American labs wage their war for supremacy
grammarElements.label: 양보 시간절 (even as)

grammarElements.text: China's internet giants
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: have opened a second front
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: by handing out $1.2 billion in subsidies ... to lure consumers toward agentic chatbots
grammarElements.label: 수단 전치사구

vocabulary.example: The battle for AI supremacy now spans three continents.

expressions.usage: 군사 용어에서 차용하여 새로운 경쟁 영역이 등장했음을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: Even as [Actor A] wages [conflict], [Actor B] has opened a second front by [strategy]
patterns.explanation: 기존 갈등이 진행 중인 가운데 새로운 경쟁 축이 열렸음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Even as regulators wage their campaign against monopolies, startups have opened a second front by disrupting from below.
Even as the company fights lawsuits, competitors have opened a second front by undercutting prices.

drillQuestions.question: China's internet giants have opened a second ______ by handing out $1.2 billion in subsidies.

sentences.notes: even as 양보절이 미중 동시 진행을 보여주고, 'second front'라는 군사 비유가 경쟁 확대를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: even as American labs wage their war for supremacy
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 연구소들이 패권 전쟁을 벌이는 가운데

koreanAlignment.en: have opened a second front
agentic chatbots capable of ordering meals and facilitating transactions
koreanAlignment.ko: 식사 주문과 거래 처리가 가능한 에이전트형 챗봇

koreanAlignment.en: an AI-powered super app

sentences.original: The ferocity of this global competition has left investors bewildered, unable to decide whether individual companies are AI winners or losers -- a confusion illustrated by Duolingo's share price doubling and then crashing by 80 percent within eighteen months, by Alphabet swinging from supposed obsolescence to an 85 percent stock surge, and by Goldman Sachs's index of firms most at risk from AI disruption falling more than 20 percent even as broader markets hovered near record highs.
sentences.translation: 이 글로벌 경쟁의 격렬함은 투자자들을 당혹스럽게 만들어 개별 기업이 AI의 승자인지 패자인지 판단하지 못하게 했다 -- 듀오링고 주가가 18개월 내에 두 배로 올랐다 80퍼센트 폭락한 것, 알파벳이 도태 추정에서 85퍼센트 주가 급등으로 뒤바뀐 것, 골드만삭스의 AI 파괴 위험 기업 지수가 광범위한 시장이 사상 최고치 근처를 맴도는 와중에 20퍼센트 이상 하락한 것이 그 혼란을 잘 보여준다.

grammarElements.text: The ferocity of this global competition
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has left investors bewildered
grammarElements.label: 사역동사 구조 (leave + O + C)

grammarElements.text: unable to decide whether ...
grammarElements.label: 형용사구 보어

grammarElements.text: a confusion illustrated by ... , by ... , and by ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격 + 병렬 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Investors were bewildered by the contradictory signals from the market.

expressions.usage: 한때 도태될 것이라 여겨졌으나 그렇지 않았음을 아이러니하게 표현

patterns.pattern: [Force] has left [group] bewildered, unable to [decide/judge] -- a confusion illustrated by [example 1], by [example 2], and by [example 3]
patterns.explanation: 혼란의 원인을 제시한 뒤 구체적 사례 세 가지로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: The regulatory shift has left banks bewildered, unable to plan ahead -- a confusion illustrated by contradictory rulings, by abrupt policy reversals, and by record compliance costs.

drillQuestions.question: The ferocity of this global competition has left investors ______, unable to decide whether companies are winners or losers.

sentences.notes: leave + O + C 사역구조 후 대시 뒤 세 가지 사례를 병렬 나열해 시장 혼란의 구체적 증거를 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The ferocity of this global competition
has left investors bewildered
supposed obsolescence
broader markets hovered near record highs
koreanAlignment.ko: 광범위한 시장이 사상 최고치 근처를 맴돌았다

sentences.original: History suggests that such confusion is entirely normal during technological revolutions, since stockmarkets correctly identify structural disruption only about half the time -- failing to foresee Kodak's demise while wrongly predicting the death of tobacco firms on multiple occasions -- and academic research confirms that analysts systematically overestimate the future profitability of companies whose technological base is becoming obsolete.
sentences.translation: 역사는 이런 혼란이 기술 혁명 시기에 전적으로 정상적이라고 시사한다. 주식시장은 구조적 파괴를 절반 정도의 확률로만 정확히 식별하기 때문이다 -- 코닥의 소멸을 예견하지 못한 반면 담배 기업의 사망을 여러 차례 잘못 예측했으며 -- 학계 연구도 분석가들이 기술 기반이 쇠퇴하는 기업의 미래 수익성을 체계적으로 과대평가한다고 확인한다.

grammarElements.text: History
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: suggests that
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: such confusion is entirely normal during technological revolutions
grammarElements.label: that절 내용

grammarElements.text: since stockmarkets correctly identify structural disruption only about half the time
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (since)

vocabulary.example: New AI tools threaten to make entire software categories obsolete.

expressions.phrase: correctly identify ... only about half the time
expressions.usage: 예측 능력의 한계를 정량적으로 보여줄 때 사용

patterns.pattern: History suggests that [current phenomenon] is entirely normal, since [precedent/evidence]
patterns.explanation: 현재 상황을 역사적 맥락에 놓아 정상화하는 패턴
examples: History suggests that market panic is entirely normal after a central bank pivot, since interest rate cycles have always produced volatility.
History suggests that regulatory backlash is entirely normal after rapid innovation, since every major technology has faced pushback.

drillQuestions.question: History suggests that such confusion is entirely normal during technological revolutions, since stockmarkets correctly identify structural disruption only about ______ the time.

sentences.notes: since절의 통계적 근거와 대시 안의 구체적 사례(코닥, 담배)가 시장의 예측 실패를 역사적으로 정상화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: History suggests that such confusion is entirely normal
koreanAlignment.ko: 역사는 이런 혼란이 전적으로 정상적이라고 시사한다

koreanAlignment.en: correctly identify structural disruption only about half the time
koreanAlignment.ko: 구조적 파괴를 절반 정도만 정확히 식별하다

koreanAlignment.en: analysts systematically overestimate the future profitability

sentences.original: India's $315-billion IT outsourcing industry, long pegged as the sector most vulnerable to AI-driven displacement, encapsulates this uncertainty perfectly: although tools like Claude Code can now prototype a software application in minutes, the messy reality of legacy systems, undocumented code, and brownfield environments means that clients who expected radical automation often end up hiring as many outsourced engineers as before, while the consultancy arms of firms like Infosys and TCS pivot toward higher-value strategic roles that could be worth $300-400 billion by decade's end.
sentences.translation: 오랫동안 AI 주도 대체에 가장 취약한 부문으로 지목되어 온 인도의 3,150억 달러 규모 IT 아웃소싱 산업은 이 불확실성을 완벽하게 요약한다. Claude Code 같은 도구가 이제 소프트웨어 애플리케이션을 몇 분 만에 시제품으로 만들 수 있지만, 레거시 시스템과 문서화되지 않은 코드, 브라운필드 환경의 지저분한 현실 때문에 급진적 자동화를 기대한 고객들은 결국 이전과 같은 수의 외주 엔지니어를 고용하게 되며, 한편 인포시스와 TCS 같은 기업의 컨설팅 부문은 2030년대 말까지 3,000~4,000억 달러 가치가 될 수 있는 고부가가치 전략적 역할로 전환하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: India's $315-billion IT outsourcing industry
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: long pegged as the sector most vulnerable to AI-driven displacement
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: encapsulates
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: although tools like Claude Code can now prototype ... the messy reality ... means that ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 양보-주절 구조 (although ... means)

vocabulary.example: The company's struggles encapsulate the broader challenges facing the industry.

expressions.usage: IT에서 깨끗한 신규 환경(greenfield)의 반대로, 기존 코드와 시스템이 복잡하게 얽힌 현실을 지칭

patterns.pattern: [Case study] encapsulates [broader trend]: although [new capability], [messy reality] means that [unexpected outcome]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사례가 더 큰 추세를 대표한다고 제시한 뒤, 기대와 현실의 괴리를 보여주는 패턴
examples: The bank's AI rollout encapsulates the industry's dilemma: although chatbots handle routine queries, the complexity of compliance means that human oversight remains essential.

drillQuestions.question: India's IT outsourcing industry ______ this uncertainty perfectly.

sentences.notes: although절과 means that절의 대비가 AI 자동화의 기대와 현실 사이 간극을 날카롭게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: long pegged as the sector most vulnerable to AI-driven displacement
koreanAlignment.ko: 오랫동안 AI 주도 대체에 가장 취약한 부문으로 지목된

koreanAlignment.en: encapsulates this uncertainty perfectly
the messy reality of legacy systems, undocumented code, and brownfield environments
koreanAlignment.ko: 레거시 시스템, 문서화되지 않은 코드, 브라운필드 환경의 지저분한 현실

koreanAlignment.en: pivot toward higher-value strategic roles

sentences.original: Whether the ultimate victors of this arms race will be the three lab founders preparing their colossal IPOs, the Chinese giants subsidising their way to dominance, or the already-profitable Alphabet -- which generated $132 billion in net income last year and can fund its AI investments from its own pocket at a comparatively modest valuation of nine times revenue -- remains a question that neither markets nor the technologists building the models can yet answer with any confidence.
sentences.translation: 이 군비 경쟁의 최종 승자가 거대 IPO를 준비하는 세 연구소 창립자인지, 보조금으로 지배력에 이르려는 중국 대기업인지, 아니면 지난해 순이익 1,320억 달러를 올리며 매출의 9배라는 비교적 소박한 밸류에이션으로 자체 자금으로 AI 투자를 감당할 수 있는 이미 흑자인 알파벳인지는, 시장도 모델을 만드는 기술자도 아직 자신 있게 답할 수 없는 질문으로 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: Whether the ultimate victors ... will be the three lab founders ..., the Chinese giants ..., or the already-profitable Alphabet
grammarElements.label: Whether 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: remains
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a question that neither markets nor the technologists ... can yet answer with any confidence
grammarElements.label: 보어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: which generated $132 billion in net income ... and can fund its AI investments from its own pocket
grammarElements.label: 삽입 관계절

vocabulary.example: The colossal scale of the offerings left underwriters scrambling.

expressions.usage: 외부 자금 조달 없이 자사의 이익으로 투자할 수 있음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Whether [option A], [option B], or [option C] remains a question that [no one] can yet answer
patterns.explanation: 열린 결말의 불확실성을 여러 선택지와 함께 제시하는 마무리 패턴
examples: Whether the future belongs to incumbents, challengers, or regulators remains a question that no analyst can yet answer.
Whether reform will come from above, below, or outside remains a question that history has not yet settled.

drillQuestions.question: Whether the ultimate victors of this arms race will be the lab founders, the Chinese giants, or Alphabet remains a question that neither markets nor technologists can yet answer with any ______.

sentences.notes: Whether절이 세 경쟁 축을 제시하고, remains a question으로 열린 결말을 만드는 전형적 분석 기사 마무리이다.

koreanAlignment.en: the ultimate victors of this arms race
the Chinese giants subsidising their way to dominance
the already-profitable Alphabet
remains a question that neither markets nor the technologists can yet answer
koreanAlignment.ko: 시장도 기술자도 아직 답할 수 없는 질문으로 남아 있다
